plan: Read existing dependency files: src/permissions/permissions-adapter.ts (US-021), src/scoring/permission-debt-score.ts (US-022), src/ui/receipt.ts, and src/modules/fingerprint/ to confirm exact interfaces and rendering patterns

Create src/modules/permission-debt/permission-debt-module.ts — async render function that: (1) calls checkPermissions() to get current permission states, (2) calls calculatePermissionDebtScore() to compute debt, (3) builds a receipt card showing Notifications, Camera, Microphone, Clipboard, and Location rows with state badges (granted/denied/prompt/unsupported), (4) displays the Permission Debt score prominently, (5) adds explanatory text about what permission debt means and why accumulated permissions increase risk

Create src/modules/permission-debt/format-permissions.ts — helper to map raw PermissionCheckResult[] into display rows with human-readable labels, state-based color coding using design tokens (--color-success for denied/prompt, --color-danger for granted, --color-text-muted for unsupported), and filtering to only show supported permissions where possible per ticket notes

Create src/modules/permission-debt/permission-debt.css — scoped styles for the module: debt score display (prominent number), permission state badges, explanatory text styling, using CSS variables from tokens.css, with reduced-motion support
Create src/modules/permission-debt/index.ts — barrel export of renderPermissionDebtModule
Wire the module into src/app/main.ts — import and append renderPermissionDebtModule() to the #dashboard element, following the same pattern as fingerprint receipt mounting

Create tests/us-023/permission-debt-module.test.ts — unit tests covering: (a) renders all 5 required permissions (notifications, camera, microphone, clipboard, location), (b) displays permission debt score, (c) shows explanatory text, (d) handles unsupported permissions gracefully, (e) returns valid HTMLElement with correct structure, (f) state badges show correct text for each PermissionState value
Run npm run lint, npm run typecheck, and npm run test to validate all checks pass
testing_recommendations: Unit test that renderPermissionDebtModule returns an HTMLElement containing rows for all 5 required permissions: notifications, camera, microphone, clipboard, location
Unit test that the debt score value from calculatePermissionDebtScore is rendered in the output
Unit test that explanatory text is present in the rendered module
Unit test with mocked navigator.permissions.query returning different states (granted, denied, prompt) to verify correct badge rendering
Unit test that unsupported permissions are handled gracefully (filtered out or shown as 'unsupported')
Unit test the format-permissions helper independently to verify label mapping and state formatting
Run full suite: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test
risks: Clipboard permission ('clipboard-read'/'clipboard-write') may not be queryable via Permissions API in all browsers — need feature detection and graceful fallback to 'unsupported'
The module is async (checkPermissions returns a Promise) which means the dashboard mounting logic needs to handle async module rendering — verify main.ts can await or handle the returned promise
Permission state badges need careful color choices to avoid implying granted=good (it should actually signal higher debt/risk) — ensure UX communicates that granted permissions increase debt
CSS scoping — ensure permission-debt styles don't leak to other modules; use specific class prefixes or CSS modules
